title: festuca marginata subsp. andresmolinae
intro: Festuca marginata subsp. andresmolinae, also known as Andes fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant is native to the Andes mountain range and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The medicinal properties of Festuca marginata subsp. andresmolinae have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of infusions or decoctions, often combined with other medicinal plants to enhance its effects.
